profile_basis: Ready [perf][no-gaps] bead frankenjax-5vqfx targeted rank-2 F64 GEMM/Dot after prior profile and pass110 comment identified dot_256x256x256 as the residual GEMM path. Fresh RCH baseline also measured raw matmul_2d_256x256x256_f64 as a control.

rejected_probe.result: rejected before commit; same original ts2 baseline eval/dot 7.3707 ms and matmul_2d 4.5660 ms, candidate eval/dot 7.3507 ms and matmul_2d 4.5113 ms, below keep threshold

kept_lever: rank-2 F64 Dot now keeps matmul_2d output in dense F64 LiteralBuffer storage instead of converting the returned Vec<f64> into Vec<Literal>. dot_f64_elements also borrows already-dense F64 inputs with Cow and keeps literal-backed inputs on the existing checked copy path.
baseline_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-5vqfx-dot-dense-control-baseline cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'dot_256x256x256_f64|matmul_2d_256x256x256_f64' --sample-size 20 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
rebench_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-5vqfx-dot-dense-final-rebench cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'dot_256x256x256_f64|matmul_2d_256x256x256_f64' --sample-size 20 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot

benchmarks.note: Raw matmul control was slightly slower in the candidate run, so the eval/dot win is not explained by faster worker/runtime conditions.

isomorphism_proof: The accepted rank-2 F64 fast path still calls matmul_2d with the same m, k, n, lhs, and rhs slices. matmul_2d preserves each output cell's ascending-k accumulation order, so floating-point rounding and NaN/inf propagation are unchanged.

Literal-backed F64 inputs still scan tensor.elements in row-major order and reject malformed non-F64 literals by returning None, which falls back to the generic dot path. Dense-backed F64 inputs borrow the same row-major f64 slice instead of copying it.

The output tensor shape and dtype are unchanged. For non-scalar rank-2 outputs, the same Vec<f64> returned by matmul_2d is wrapped with TensorValue::new_f64_values instead of first mapping each value through Literal::from_f64 and TensorValue::new.

LiteralBuffer equality, indexing, iteration, IntoIterator, Debug, and Serialize all materialize or view the same ordered Literal sequence via as_slice(), so observable element order and JSON serialization are unchanged. The golden freezes the output bits for a 3x4 @ 4x2 dot case.
No RNG, tie-breaking, sorting, or transform ordering is touched.
validation: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-5vqfx-dot-dense-tests cargo test -p fj-lax dot_rank2 --lib -- --nocapture (3 passed)
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-5vqfx-dot-dense-check cargo check -p fj-lax --all-targets
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-5vqfx-dot-dense-libtest cargo test -p fj-lax --lib (1082 passed)
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-5vqfx-dot-dense-clippy cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings
